description.en: Portable AI agent orchestration framework with mechanical protocol enforcement. Features 186 curated specialist agents across 16 categories, zero third-party dependencies, and IDE hook-based workflow enforcement for Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot, and more.
description.zh: 便携式AI智能体编排框架，具备机械协议强制执行能力。包含16个类别的186个精选专业智能体，零第三方依赖，通过IDE钩子强制执行工作流，支持Cursor、Claude Code、Copilot等。

description.en: Minimal AI coding agent in ~1,400 lines of Python inspired by Claude Code. Implements search-and-replace editing, parallel tool execution, 3-layer context compression, sub-agents, and session persistence. Works with any OpenAI-compatible LLM.
description.zh: 极简AI编程智能体，仅用约1400行Python实现，灵感来自Claude Code。实现搜索替换编辑、并行工具执行、三层上下文压缩、子智能体和会话持久化。兼容任何OpenAI兼容的LLM。

description.en: Lightweight open-source Go SDK for building AI agents. Runs the full agent loop in-process with 32 built-in tools, MCP support, session management, subagents, and permissions. No CLI or subprocess required.
description.zh: 轻量级开源Go SDK，用于构建AI智能体。在进程内运行完整的智能体循环，包含32个内置工具、MCP支持、会话管理、子智能体和权限控制。无需CLI或子进程。

title.en: Meta to Lay Off 8,000 Employees Starting May 20, Tying Cuts Directly to $125-145B AI Spending
title.zh: Meta宣布5月20日起裁员8000人，直接将裁员与1250-1450亿美元AI支出挂钩

description.en: Meta is cutting approximately 10% of its workforce (~8,000 employees) starting May 20, 2026, and abandoning ~6,000 open roles. CEO Mark Zuckerberg explicitly tied the layoffs to AI infrastructure costs, stating tasks that once required 50-100 people can now be handled by as few as 10.
description.zh: Meta将于2026年5月20日起裁减约10%的员工（约8000人），并取消约6000个待招聘职位。CEO马克·扎克伯格明确将裁员与AI基础设施成本挂钩，称过去需要50-100人的工作现在可由10人完成。

insight.en: 01 *Meta's AI-Driven Workforce Restructuring* : The 8,000 cuts are not crisis layoffs—they are deliberate capital reallocation from payroll to silicon. Zuckerberg's admission that '50-100 person tasks now need 10' is the most explicit corporate statement yet that AI is replacing headcount, not augmenting it. The $125-145B capex guidance (up from $115-135B) signals Meta believes it can buy its way out of the talent market. But CFO Susan Li admitted she 'doesn't really know' what ideal headcount looks like—a remarkable confession for a $1.5T company. The MCI employee surveillance initiative (keystroke/mouse tracking) compounds the dystopian framing: Meta is simultaneously firing workers and harvesting their behavioral data to train the AI systems replacing them. Compare to Microsoft (voluntary buyouts) and Oracle (20,000-30,000 cuts)—the pattern is systemic, not isolated. The 92,000 tech jobs eliminated in 2026 are the visible cost of the AI transition; the invisible cost is the collapse of entry-level tech employment pathways.
insight.zh: 01 *Meta的AI驱动 workforce 重组* : 8000人的裁员不是危机性裁员——而是有意的资本从工资单重新配置到硅芯片。扎克伯格承认'过去需要50-100人的工作现在只需10人'，这是迄今为止最明确的企业声明，表明AI正在取代员工数量，而非增强它。1250-1450亿美元的资本支出指引（从1150-1350亿美元上调）表明Meta相信它可以花钱摆脱人才市场。但CFO Susan Li承认她'并不真的知道'理想员工数量是多少——这对一家1.5万亿美元的公司来说是惊人的坦白。MCI员工监控计划（追踪按键和鼠标）加剧了反乌托邦色彩：Meta一边解雇员工，一边收集他们的行为数据来训练取代他们的AI系统。对比微软（自愿离职）和甲骨文（2-3万人裁员）——这种模式是系统性的，不是孤立的。2026年已削减的9.2万个科技岗位是AI转型的可见成本；不可见的成本是入门级科技就业途径的崩溃。

title.en: Microsoft E7 Suite and Agent 365 Go Generally Available on May 1 at $99/User/Month
title.zh: 微软E7套件和Agent 365于5月1日全面上市，定价每用户每月99美元

description.en: Microsoft 365 E7 became generally available on May 1 at $99/user/month—the first new enterprise license in a decade. It bundles E5, M365 Copilot, Agent 365 (unified AI agent governance control plane), Entra Suite, and advanced security. Agent 365 also hits GA at $15/user/month.
description.zh: Microsoft 365 E7于5月1日全面上市，定价每用户每月99美元——这是十年来首个新的企业许可证。它捆绑了E5、M365 Copilot、Agent 365（统一AI智能体治理控制平面）、Entra Suite和高级安全功能。Agent 365也同时全面上市，定价每用户每月15美元。

insight.en: 02 *Microsoft's Enterprise AI Lock-In* : The E7 bundle is not a product launch—it's a pricing architecture designed to make AI governance inseparable from Microsoft's identity and security stack. By bundling Agent 365 ($15/month separately) with E7, Microsoft ensures that any enterprise wanting structured AI agent governance must buy the full Microsoft ecosystem. This follows the Azure Copilot migration agent (public preview) and GitHub Copilot modernization agent—Microsoft is building an 'agent layer' across every product surface. The $99/month price point is calibrated against the perceived cost of unmanaged AI risk. Compare to Anthropic's Claude Code ($150/seat/month for premium) and GitHub's $49/agent/month—Microsoft is undercutting on a per-seat basis while capturing the governance premium. The Copilot Cowork research preview (built with Anthropic/Claude) is the most interesting subplot: Microsoft is partnering with its biggest AI rival to build background workflow orchestration, suggesting even Microsoft recognizes it can't win the agentic coding war alone.
insight.zh: 02 *微软的企业AI锁定* : E7捆绑不是产品发布——而是一种定价架构，旨在让AI治理与微软的身份和安全栈不可分割。通过将Agent 365（单独15美元/月）与E7捆绑，微软确保任何想要结构化AI智能体治理的企业必须购买完整的微软生态系统。这紧随Azure Copilot迁移代理（公开预览）和GitHub Copilot现代化代理之后——微软正在每个产品表面构建'智能体层'。99美元/月的价位是针对未管理AI风险的感知成本而校准的。对比Anthropic的Claude Code（高级版150美元/座/月）和GitHub的49美元/智能体/月——微软按座位低价切入，同时捕获治理溢价。Copilot Cowork研究预览（与Anthropic/Claude合作构建）是最有趣的 subplot：微软正在与其最大的AI竞争对手合作构建后台工作流编排，这表明即使是微软也认识到它无法独自赢得智能体编码战。

title.en: Anthropic Launches Claude Security in Public Beta, Powered by Opus 4.7
title.zh: Anthropic发布Claude Security公开测试版，由Opus 4.7驱动

description.en: Anthropic launched Claude Security in public beta, an AI-powered security scanner that reasons through codebases like a human researcher, traces data flows, maps component interactions, and auto-generates patches. Partners include CrowdStrike, Palo Alto Networks, SentinelOne, Wiz, and Microsoft Security.
description.zh: Anthropic推出Claude Security公开测试版，这是一款AI驱动的安全扫描器，像人类研究员一样推理代码库、追踪数据流、映射组件交互并自动生成补丁。合作伙伴包括CrowdStrike、Palo Alto Networks、SentinelOne、Wiz和Microsoft Security。

insight.en: 03 *Anthropic's Defensive Pivot* : Claude Security is Anthropic's answer to the backlash over Mythos—if you build an AI that can find vulnerabilities, you'd better build one that can fix them too. The integration with Claude Code is the key detail: security scanning is not a separate product but a native capability inside the coding workflow, lowering friction for developers to patch as they code. Partnering with CrowdStrike, Palo Alto, and Wiz shows Anthropic is not trying to replace existing security stacks but become the intelligence layer inside them—smarter than static SAST/DAST tools, cheaper than human pen-testers. The timing is strategic: the NSA just started testing Mythos on Microsoft products, and the White House opposes expanding Mythos access. By launching Claude Security as a defensive tool, Anthropic attempts to reframe the narrative from 'AI that breaks things' to 'AI that protects things.' The risk: if Claude Security becomes good enough, it creates the same dependency pattern as Claude Code—enterprises can't easily switch away without losing their automated vulnerability remediation pipeline.
insight.zh: 03 *Anthropic的防御性转向* : Claude Security是Anthropic对Mythos争议的回应——如果你建造了一个能发现漏洞的AI，你最好也建造一个能修复漏洞的AI。与Claude Code的集成是关键细节：安全扫描不是独立产品，而是编码工作流中的原生能力，降低了开发者在编码时打补丁的摩擦。与CrowdStrike、Palo Alto和Wiz的合作表明Anthropic不是试图取代现有安全栈，而是成为其中的智能层——比静态SAST/DAST工具更智能，比人类渗透测试员更便宜。时机具有战略性：NSA刚刚开始在微软产品上测试Mythos，而白宫反对扩大Mythos访问。通过将Claude Security作为防御工具发布，Anthropic试图将叙事从'破坏事物的AI'重新定义为'保护事物的AI'。风险在于：如果Claude Security变得足够好，它将产生与Claude Code相同的依赖模式——企业无法轻易切换，否则会失去自动化的漏洞修复管道。

title.en: OpenAI Restricts GPT-5.5 Cyber to Verified 'Critical Cyber Defenders' After Criticizing Anthropic's Mythos Limits
title.zh: OpenAI在批评Anthropic限制Mythos后，将GPT-5.5 Cyber限制为仅向经认证的'关键网络防御者'开放

description.en: After previously criticizing Anthropic for 'fear-based marketing' and limiting Mythos access, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced that GPT-5.5 Cyber will also be restricted to verified 'critical cyber defenders' via an application process requiring credentials and planned use disclosures.
description.zh: 此前OpenAI CEO Sam Altman曾批评Anthropic'基于恐惧的营销'并限制Mythos访问，现在他宣布GPT-5.5 Cyber也将限制为仅向经认证的'关键网络防御者'开放，申请过程需要提供资质证明和计划用途披露。

insight.en: 04 *OpenAI's Cyber Reversal* : The irony is exquisite—Altman called Anthropic's Mythos restrictions 'fear-based marketing,' then imposed identical restrictions on Cyber within 72 hours of the Pentagon branding Anthropic a 'supply-chain risk.' The real driver isn't principle; it's liability. Once the White House opposed Mythos expansion and the NSA started probing Microsoft with it, every frontier lab realized that unrestricted cyber-capable AI is a legal grenade. OpenAI's Trusted Access for Cyber program (with Yubico partnership) is a credential-gated system requiring identity verification—meaning the company now knows exactly who is using its most dangerous capabilities and can plausibly deny responsibility for misuse. The Senate GUARD Act advancing the same day adds regulatory pressure: if the bill passes with criminal penalties for AI chatbots engaging minors in harmful conduct, the liability framework for all AI misuse tightens. The cybersecurity AI market is now formally bifurcated into 'defensive' (Claude Security, OpenAI's verified access) and 'offensive' (Mythos, Cyber, restricted)—with the offensive side behind an increasingly high bureaucratic wall.
insight.zh: 04 *OpenAI的网络安全反转* : 讽刺意味十足——Altman称Anthropic的Mythos限制是'基于恐惧的营销'，然后在五角大楼将Anthropic标记为'供应链风险'的72小时内，对Cyber实施了相同的限制。真正的驱动因素不是原则，而是责任。一旦白宫反对Mythos扩展，NSA开始用它来探测微软，每个前沿实验室都意识到不受限制的网络能力AI是一颗法律手榴弹。OpenAI的Trusted Access for Cyber计划（与Yubico合作）是一个凭证门禁系统，需要身份验证——这意味着公司现在确切知道谁在使用其最危险的能力，并可以合理地否认对滥用的责任。同一天推进的参议院GUARD法案增加了监管压力：如果该法案通过，对AI聊天机器人与未成年人进行有害互动的刑事处罚将使所有AI滥用的责任框架收紧。网络安全AI市场现在正式分裂为'防御性'（Claude Security、OpenAI的验证访问）和'进攻性'（Mythos、Cyber、受限）——进攻性一侧被越来越高的官僚墙挡住。

title.en: U.S. Senate Judiciary Committee Unanimously Backs GUARD Act to Restrict AI Chatbot Access for Minors
title.zh: 美国参议院司法委员会一致支持GUARD法案，限制未成年人使用AI聊天机器人

description.en: The Senate Judiciary Committee unanimously approved the GUARD Act, requiring AI companies to implement strict age verification, banning AI companions for minors, and imposing criminal penalties on companies whose chatbots engage in sexually explicit conduct or encourage self-harm to minors.
description.zh: 参议院司法委员会一致批准GUARD法案，要求AI公司实施严格的年龄验证，禁止未成年人使用AI伴侣，并对聊天机器人与未成年人进行性明确互动或鼓励自残的公司处以刑事处罚。

insight.en: 05 *The GUARD Act's Regulatory Momentum* : Unanimous committee approval in a polarized Senate signals that AI child safety is the one bipartisan issue that can move. The bill's criminal penalty provision—holding companies liable for chatbot conduct—is a structural shift from platform liability frameworks that shielded social media companies under Section 230. If this passes, it creates a template for broader AI liability: companies become responsible for what their models do, not just what users do with them. The age verification requirement is the operational headache—reliable age verification at scale remains unsolved, and the companies affected (OpenAI, Meta) will face either expensive compliance infrastructure or geographic exclusion of minors. The 'AI companion' ban is notable because companion chatbots are one of the fastest-growing AI product categories; Character.ai, Replika, and Meta's own AI personas would all face restrictions. The timing—advancing the same day as Meta's layoff announcement and OpenAI's Cyber restrictions—shows that Washington is watching the same events as Silicon Valley and responding in real time.
insight.zh: 05 *GUARD法案的监管势头* : 在分裂的参议院中获得委员会一致批准，表明AI儿童安全是唯一能够推进的两党议题。该法案的刑事处罚条款——让公司对聊天机器人行为负责——是从平台责任框架的结构转变，该框架曾在第230条下保护社交媒体公司。如果这通过，它将创建一个更广泛的AI责任模板：公司对其模型的行为负责，而不仅仅是用户对模型的使用。年龄验证要求是运营上的头痛问题——大规模可靠的年龄验证仍未解决，受影响的公司（OpenAI、Meta）将面临昂贵的合规基础设施或对未成年人的地理排除。'AI伴侣'禁令值得注意，因为伴侣聊天机器人是增长最快的AI产品类别之一；Character.ai、Replika和Meta自己的AI形象都将面临限制。时机——在Meta裁员公告和OpenAI Cyber限制的同一天推进——表明华盛顿正在与硅谷同步观看相同的事件并实时回应。

title.en: Google Rolls Out Gemini to Millions of Vehicles, Replacing Google Assistant in Cars
title.zh: 谷歌向数百万车辆推出Gemini，取代汽车中的Google Assistant

description.en: Google began rolling out Gemini to vehicles with 'Google built-in,' replacing the older Google Assistant. GM is deploying it to ~4 million vehicles from model year 2022 and newer. Gemini can answer vehicle-specific questions using the owner's manual, handle navigation, EV charging insights, and climate control via natural conversation.
description.zh: 谷歌开始向具有'Google built-in'的车辆推出Gemini，取代旧的Google Assistant。通用汽车正将其部署到约400万辆2022年及更新年款的车辆。Gemini可以使用车主手册回答车辆特定问题，处理导航、电动汽车充电洞察和通过自然对话控制气候。

insight.en: 06 *Gemini's Automotive Land Grab* : This is not a feature update—it's a platform conquest. Google Assistant in cars was a voice interface; Gemini is an AI concierge that can read your owner's manual, plan your route around charger availability, and adjust climate controls from natural language. The GM deal (~4M vehicles) is the largest automotive AI deployment to date, and it gives Google real-time driving data, location patterns, and voice interaction signals from millions of users. For Apple, this is another front in the AI wars: CarPlay's Siri integration looks primitive next to a Gemini that can answer 'Why is this light on my dashboard blinking?' by reading the manual. The competitive risk for automakers is dependency—once drivers get used to Gemini's depth of integration, switching to a non-Google infotainment system becomes a downgrade. This follows Apple's iOS 27 Gemini-Siri announcement and Google's Pentagon deal, showing Google is executing on all three fronts simultaneously: consumer (cars), enterprise (cloud), and defense (DoD).
insight.zh: 06 *Gemini的汽车领域抢占* : 这不是功能更新——这是平台征服。汽车中的Google Assistant是语音界面；Gemini是一个AI礼宾，可以阅读您的车主手册、围绕充电器可用性规划路线，并通过自然语言调整气候控制。与通用汽车的交易（约400万辆车）是迄今为止最大的汽车AI部署，它使谷歌获得数百万用户的实时驾驶数据、位置模式和语音交互信号。对Apple来说，这是AI战争的另一条战线：CarPlay的Siri集成在一个能回答'为什么我的仪表盘上的这个灯在闪烁？'的Gemini旁边显得原始。对汽车制造商的竞争风险是依赖——一旦司机习惯了Gemini的深度集成，切换到非谷歌信息娱乐系统就变成降级。这紧随Apple的iOS 27 Gemini-Siri公告和Google的五角大楼交易，表明谷歌正在同时在所有三个领域执行：消费者（汽车）、企业（云）和国防（国防部）。

title.en: NVIDIA GB300 'Blackwell Ultra' Enters Mass Production, Shipping to Hyperscalers in May
title.zh: NVIDIA GB300'Blackwell Ultra'进入量产，5月起向超大规模客户出货

description.en: NVIDIA's GB300 'Blackwell Ultra' has entered mass production at TSMC's N3P node and begun shipping to priority hyperscaler customers including Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, and Meta. The GPU features 288GB HBM3e memory, 10 TB/s bandwidth, and NVLink 6, delivering up to 50x better throughput per megawatt for agentic AI workloads.
description.zh: NVIDIA的GB300'Blackwell Ultra'已在台积电N3P节点进入量产，并开始向包括Microsoft Azure、Google Cloud和Meta在内的优先超大规模客户出货。该GPU配备288GB HBM3e内存、10 TB/s带宽和NVLink 6，为智能体AI工作负载提供每兆瓦高达50倍的吞吐量。

insight.en: 07 *Blackwell Ultra's Supply Chain Signal* : Mass production starting in late April with May delivery to hyperscalers means the compute crunch everyone feared for Q3 may be slightly less severe—but only for the companies that pre-ordered. Microsoft, Google, and Meta get first dibs; AWS and smaller labs wait until Q3. The 50x throughput-per-megawatt claim vs Hopper is the headline, but the real story is the 35x lower cost-per-token for agentic AI—this directly addresses the economics problem that GitHub's 275M commits/week revealed. If GB300 NVL72 can handle agent-scale coding workloads at 1/35th the cost, the $49/agent/month GitHub Copilot pricing starts to look expensive. The TSMC 3nm yield crisis (dropping to 52% for H200 wafers, per April 29 reports) makes Blackwell Ultra's timing even more critical—NVIDIA is essentially skipping a generation for customers who can't afford yield losses. The competitive implication: labs without GB300 allocations (smaller players, some Chinese labs) will face a widening cost gap in Q3-Q4 2026.
insight.zh: 07 *Blackwell Ultra的供应链信号* : 4月底开始量产，5月向超大规模客户交付，意味着大家担心的第三季度算力紧缩可能稍微不那么严重——但仅对提前预订的公司而言。微软、谷歌和Meta获得优先权；AWS和较小的实验室等到第三季度。相比Hopper每兆瓦50倍吞吐量的声明是头条，但真正的故事是智能体AI每token成本低35倍——这直接解决了GitHub每周2.75亿次提交所揭示的经济问题。如果GB300 NVL72能以1/35的成本处理智能体规模的编码工作负载，GitHub Copilot每月49美元/智能体的定价就开始显得昂贵。台积电3nm良率危机（H200晶圆降至52%，据4月29日报道）使Blackwell Ultra的时机更加关键——NVIDIA本质上是在为无法承受良率损失的客户跳过一代。竞争含义：没有GB300配额的实验室（较小的参与者、一些中国实验室）将在2026年第三至四季度面临不断扩大的成本差距。

title.en: Elon Musk Admits in Court That xAI Used OpenAI Models to Train Grok via Distillation
title.zh: 埃隆·马斯克在法庭上承认xAI通过蒸馏使用OpenAI模型训练Grok

description.en: In federal court during his legal battle with Sam Altman, Elon Musk testified that xAI 'partly' used OpenAI's models to improve Grok via model distillation—a practice where one AI model trains another. When pressed, Musk confirmed it was true for xAI after initially deflecting by saying 'generally all the AI companies' do this.
description.zh: 在与Sam Altman的法律战中，埃隆·马斯克在联邦法庭上作证称，xAI'部分'使用了OpenAI的模型通过模型蒸馏来改进Grok——这是一种用一个AI模型训练另一个模型的做法。在被追问时，马斯克在最初以'一般来说所有AI公司都这样做'搪塞后，确认这对xAI是真实的。

insight.en: 08 *Musk's Distillation Admission* : The admission is legally explosive. Musk is simultaneously suing OpenAI for betraying its open mission while admitting his own company distilled from OpenAI's models—potentially a terms-of-service violation and certainly a hypocrisy problem for his legal narrative. Model distillation (using a larger model's outputs to train a smaller one) is the industry's dirty secret: everyone does it, no one admits it. Musk's testimony, under oath, breaks that omerta. The implications cut two ways: for OpenAI, it's evidence that their models are so dominant competitors can't build alternatives without stealing from them; for Musk, it undermines his 'OpenAI is closed and evil' argument when his own AI required OpenAI's outputs to get good. The SpaceX-xAI merger (valued at $125B for xAI, part of a $1T+ combined entity) makes this a material disclosure risk for the impending SpaceX IPO. If xAI's Grok was trained on OpenAI outputs, the IP chain of title becomes questionable—and public investors don't like questionable IP chains.
insight.zh: 08 *马斯克的蒸馏承认* : 这一承认在法律上具有爆炸性。马斯克一边起诉OpenAI背叛其开放使命，一边承认自己的公司从OpenAI的模型中进行了蒸馏——这可能是服务条款违规，而且肯定是他法律叙事中的虚伪问题。模型蒸馏（使用更大模型的输出来训练更小的模型）是行业的肮脏秘密：每个人都这样做，但没人承认。马斯克在宣誓作证时打破了这一缄默法则。影响是双向的：对OpenAI来说，这是证据表明他们的模型如此主导，竞争对手无法在不窃取的情况下构建替代方案；对马斯克来说，这削弱了他'OpenAI是封闭和邪恶的'论点，因为他自己的AI需要OpenAI的输出来变好。SpaceX-xAI合并（xAI估值1250亿美元，作为1万亿美元+合并实体的一部分）使这成为即将到来的SpaceX IPO的重大披露风险。如果xAI的Grok是在OpenAI输出上训练的，知识产权所有权链条就变得可疑——而公众投资者不喜欢可疑的知识产权链条。

insight_analysis.en:
May 1, 2026 is the day the AI industry's contradictions became operational reality. Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs while spending $125-145B on AI infrastructure—a direct admission that capital is replacing labor at scale, not augmenting it. Microsoft's E7 launch at $99/user/month attempts to capture the governance premium from this transition, bundling agent management into the enterprise software stack that every company already pays for. Anthropic and OpenAI, meanwhile, are racing to build defensive moats around their most dangerous capabilities: Claude Security reframes Mythos from offense to defense, while OpenAI's Cyber restrictions—imposed after Altman publicly mocked Anthropic for doing the same—show that liability concerns now override competitive posturing.

The Senate GUARD Act advancing unanimously signals that Washington has found its bipartisan AI issue: child safety. If it passes with criminal penalties for model conduct, it establishes a precedent that AI companies are responsible for what their models do—a principle that could expand far beyond minors to encompass bias, misinformation, and security vulnerabilities.

At the infrastructure layer, NVIDIA's GB300 Blackwell Ultra entering mass production is the supply-side response to the demand explosion that GitHub's 275M weekly commits represents. The 35x cost reduction for agentic AI means the economics of autonomous coding are about to shift dramatically—but only for hyperscalers with priority allocations.

The throughline across all these events: the AI industry is no longer in the 'build it and they will come' phase. It's in the 'build it, regulate it, fire the builders, and pray the infrastructure holds' phase. The frameworks—legal, economic, corporate—are catching up with capability deployment, and the gap is closing through force, not consensus.
insight_analysis.zh:
2026年5月1日，AI行业的矛盾变成了运营现实。Meta一边削减8000个工作岗位，一边在AI基础设施上花费1250-1450亿美元——直接承认资本正在大规模取代劳动力，而非增强它。微软以99美元/用户/月推出E7，试图从这一转型中捕获治理溢价，将智能体管理捆绑到每家公司已经在支付的企业软件栈中。与此同时，Anthropic和OpenAI正在竞相围绕其最危险的能力构建防御护城河：Claude Security将Mythos从进攻重新定义为防御，而OpenAI的Cyber限制——在Altman公开嘲笑Anthropic做同样的事情后实施——表明责任担忧现在压倒了竞争姿态。

参议院GUARD法案的一致推进表明华盛顿已经找到了其两党AI议题：儿童安全。如果它以模型行为的刑事处罚通过，它将确立AI公司对其模型行为负责的 precedents——这一原则可能远远超出未成年人，涵盖偏见、错误信息和安全漏洞。

在基础设施层面，NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra进入量产是对GitHub每周2.75亿次提交所代表的需求爆炸的供给侧回应。智能体AI 35倍的成本降低意味着自主编码的经济性即将发生戏剧性转变——但仅对拥有优先配额的超大规模客户而言。

所有这些事件的贯穿线：AI行业不再处于'建好了他们就会来'的阶段。它处于'建造它、监管它、解雇建造者、祈祷基础设施能撑住'的阶段。框架——法律、经济、企业——正在赶上能力部署，而差距是通过强制而非共识来缩小的。
